CheckTitle: Ensure disabled service principals do not have active credentials
Description: Identifies service principals that are disabled (accountEnabled=false) but still have active password credentials or certificate credentials. These credentials could be re-enabled or exploited if the service principal is accidentally re-enabled.
Risk: Disabled service principals with active credentials represent latent access paths. If re-enabled accidentally or by an attacker with sufficient privileges, the existing credentials provide immediate access without requiring credential provisioning.

Remediation.Recommendation: Remove all credentials (secrets and certificates) from disabled service principals. If the service principal is no longer needed, delete it entirely.
